channel.title: SPL - High Point Branch
channel.description: Upcoming events for SPL - High Point Branch

item.title: Family Story Time
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: High Point Branch, Meeting Room

At storytime, kids ages 0-5 and their families can enjoy stories, songs, and fun learning activities. 

Event details

item.title: Homework Help
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: High Point Branch, Meeting Room

Homework Help volunteers will be at this branch to assist K-12 students with school assignments during the school year. 

Event details

item.title: Homework Help
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: High Point Branch, Meeting Room

Homework Help volunteers will be at this branch to assist K-12 students with school assignments during the school year. 

Event details

item.title: Homework Help
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: High Point Branch, Meeting Room

Homework Help volunteers will be at this branch to assist K-12 students with school assignments during the school year. 

Event details

item.title: Reduced and Free Transit Fare Enrollment
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: High Point Branch

Low-income individuals and individuals with disabilities can receive a reduced-fare ORCA LIFT transit pass.Individuals receiving TANF, RCA, PWA, SSI, HEN, or ABD can receive a subsidized year-long pass.

Event details

item.title: Youth Drop-in Chess
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: High Point Branch, Meeting Room

Youth Drop-in Chess for Children and Teens under 18 years of age. Come learn, practice and improve your chess playing skills.

Event details

item.title: Author Talk: Claudia Rowe discusses "Wards of the State: The Long Shadow of American Foster Care"
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: High Point Branch

Rowe's reporting on racially skewed school discipline for The Seattle Times helped to change education laws in Washington State, and her coverage of Latino youth gangs was nominated for a Pulitzer Prize. 

Event details

item.title: Family Story Time
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: High Point Branch, Meeting Room

At storytime, kids ages 0-5 and their families can enjoy stories, songs, and fun learning activities.
